All we need is an opportunity. And the opportunity has come.
They've taken aim.
The malice is sickening.

lines.x: They could fire at any time.
Not exactly a great feeling to be in that thing's crosshairs.
Misery?
Whenever you're ready—
Do it.

lines.st: Red.
The color on the deck flashes back before my eyes.
The demonic eye in the altar peers at me.
It is averted by a silver arc.
The dagger rips open the necks of a file of Sarkaz artillerymen, leaping back joyfully into Misery's hand.
Their hands slow for a moment as they reach for the core, continuing their movements from before they died under the pull of inertia.
A hammer falls from the sky, impacting lightly with the red wave.
Bang.
Vina is knocked back into the building like a comet.

lines.x: No... Vina!

lines.st: Half a second.
The altar activated faster than anyone expected.

lines.x: *Cough*... *cough*...

lines.st: The hammer embeds itself in the broken wall.
I can't see anything in the dust and smoke.

lines.x: Watch out! They've replaced the artillerymen and are preparing to fire again!

lines.st: The hammer is gone by the time I wipe the gravel from my eyes.
A figure climbs the outer wall swiftly, the hammer pulling her up like a pickaxe, the building trembling under every blow.

lines.x: Dagda!

lines.st: Dagda grabs the hand that stretches out from within the light.
Brick and stone crack and break under Vina's heels. She leaps into the sky.
The red wave has begun to build.

lines.x: Everyone, take cover!
Get down, Morgan!
Hngh...

lines.st: I look up in the storm.
She walks upon the rays of dawn, her golden hair burning.
She raises her hammer high, and it becomes one with the sun.
The wave is torn apart, rampant power streaking towards the core.
I lose my voice.
All that remains is the light that stabs at my eyes.

lines.x: Vina! Vina!
The altars are still exploding! Look out!
She's fainted! Damn, the metal shards got into her chest! Someone help me out!
Bring her back! Don't let Originium dust into the wounds!
The Sarkaz are retreating!
Hannah! Look! Over there!
Battleships? Reinforcements? Really? Th-That's the standard of Duke Gododdin!
It's... it's over...
It's over! We bloody made it! We did!
Ben! Ben! Look, we're saved! Ben? Ben...
Those damnable devils are trying to run! After them!

lines.st: I should add another entry to the scout's journal.
'We survived.
In memory of seven fallen heroes...
Abigail, of Norport, civilian. Alexandra, of Chetleigh, civilian.
Henderson, of Auchterigg, soldier. Bentley, of Lynncardine, soldier.
Unidentified body.
Unidentified body.
Unidentified... (illegible)'
No one can be told what a battlefield is. You have to see it for yourself.

My friends and I spent a lot of time in Norport's video shop watching old movies, many of them war movies. There were story of heroes, but also stories of terror, depicted by directors with a sadistic streak.
I remembered that the four of us all had nightmares that night.
Even now, I seem to be drifting in a dream.

lines.x: We took on a few soldiers who were still breathing when we passed through that valley. Oh, and the survivors have caught up to our column.
They're all Infected. As they should be, after spending a night in a corpse-filled crater.
You did disinfect properly, didn't you? You could have gotten Active Originium dust on you.

lines.st: Where are we, Hannah?
I'm a little...

lines.x: Don't worry, I have experience with large-scale Active Originium zones from my time in County Hillock.
We undertook the cleanup according to SOP.
The soldiers were conscripts under the Duke of Ashworth. They said that their company simply pulled out after the battle, without rescuing the wounded or cleaning up the battlefield.

lines.st: Horn? Is Hannah angry at me because I acted on my own?
Why is she ignoring me?

lines.x: He said he had a friend who had been crying for help by a shallow puddle all night. Couldn't turn himself over because his legs were injured.
He had drowned by the time we found him.
The puddle was ankle-deep. He would have survived if someone had just flipped him over.
I examined the area. Looks like they marched into a Royal Court trap. Some Sarkaz mercenaries must have sacrificed themselves by acting as bait.

lines.st: There's something on my chest?
I can't breathe... Morgan... Morgan?
I feel something hot roiling in my chest.

lines.x: But... how could they have gone down without a fight?
That's not surprising. Other than the strongest dukes, professional soldiers make up only a quarter of the armies of the other ducal armies, at best.
Most Victorian soldiers are conscripted, sent into battle after only a few months of basic training on how to use a crossbow or Arts Unit.
Frankly, if not for this war, the officers and soldiers may never have spoken to each other their entire lives.
Vina wouldn't like to hear it.
I hate this. How could you trust your back to someone whom you don't trust?

lines.st: Why does nobody hear me?
I feel the taste of iron in my mouth, something boiling, rushing up my throat.

lines.x: Mother never answered my questions. She said I would find the answer, someday—
*Cough*... argh!
Vina!
Take it easy, Morgan... my neck... I can't breathe...
Sorry, got excited. You had been unconscious for two days.
You were running a fever. At least the wound on your chest was healing well, otherwise we would have been even more worried.
Where are we...? We aren't in Chetleigh anymore, are we?

lines.st: They are silent. What happened?

lines.x: Ahem... Maybe I should explain.
We pulled out two days ago. We're back to our original encampment.
At Horn's advice, we pulled out as quickly as possible before the Duke of Gododdin's fleet entered Chetleigh, avoiding direct contact with the reinforcements.
...Horn?
We have a lot of... complicated circumstances. It's going to cause a lot of complications if we establish official communications with the dukes' troops.
I've talked about this with Misery, and the others support what we're doing.
While we still have troops formerly under the command of Duke Windermere, their military ID has expired, and there's a high risk of 'friendly fire'.
Well, not so much expired as no longer recognized...
No need to mince words about the nobles' dirty deeds.
It's the same deal as gangs fighting for territory.

lines.st: Indra's right.
Since when did the war become like this?
We could give help to where we can see, but what about the places beyond?

lines.x: ...What's the situation?
Some have chosen to join Duke Gododdin's forces and continue the fight. Gododdin's officers didn't even say anything about the Infected. The majority have chosen to stay with us, however.
They trust us. Or should I say, they trust you. We won! Morale is really high right now.
They're even joking about the old conflicts now.
We did it.
Your plan worked. We made it. It's very possible that no one will ever know the sacrifices we made...
It's just one small victory in a greater war.

lines.st: I look at Delphine's tired face. A great burden has weighed upon her heart ever since she was left behind by the battleships.
But now, I see a rare smile on her face.

lines.x: But we saved Chetleigh, Vina.
Yes, we did.

lines.st: My brain tells me that I should be relieved or happy.
But I can't do it. I'm afraid to know how many stayed behind in that building, never to leave it.
Everything that happened there seems like a dream.
Is this what victory feels like? It's so weighty.

lines.x: Would things have gotten so bad if everyone was fully committed to fighting for Victoria from the very beginning?

No, they all firmly believe that they are fighting for Victoria.
It's the other way round. Things have already spiraled out of control. Therefore...
We're fighting for ourselves now.
Siege, one of the survivors from Chetleigh has brought us a surprise.
His family collected Gaulish artifacts, and his father once picked up a little Victorian object from Lingones's former route.
It wasn't anything valuable, so it has been sitting in a warehouse for a long time. He gave it to us before we left as a gift of gratitude, and Horn recognized it immediately.
Here.
This is an IFF beacon used in IFVs. It emits a signal that allows Victorian armies to identify the bearer's affiliation.
It contains a military identification code?
Yes. This black box belonged to the Victorian Army. I'm familiar with the symbol on it.
But I thought that the unit that it once belonged to no longer exists, lost in the ruins of Gaul—
—the Exemplars.
A unit of legend, predecessor to Tempest Platoon. This thing took a long journey back to Victoria.
Back to us.

lines.st: 'The Exemplars'...
The present dukes may not recognize this ancient unit. At best, it might prevent direct artillery strikes.
But the more important thing is...
The mission of the unit was inscribed inside the box.
The Exemplars
'We make miracles.'

lines.x: ...Hah.
Consider it a gift from fate.
What's this...? The beacon that Lieutenant Shearer left us has picked up a signal.
Shearer?
Lady Delphine, there has been a change of circumstances at Irnclad Galavae...
We are approaching your position now. ETA in one hour.
Be prepared for unhappy news... and a guest who insists on seeing you.
Your wounds! What a shoddy work of first aid!
It may look like the wounds have closed, but the inflammation wasn't effectively treated, which explains the fever.
With serious wounds like these, it's important that they're treated by a doctor and all vital signs monitored, reducing the chance of lingering after-effects.
You're fortunate that it was a piece of metal, not Originium crystal. When you're dealing with Sarkaz witchcraft devices, even the slightest touch could be infectious.
Even if it wasn't directly caused by Originium, there was a high risk of infection given the circumstances. It's incredible that your blood Originium levels still remain at normal levels.
You really should thank your lucky stars.
Well, it was a chaotic situation, Lieutenant.
At least I'm alive. It pays to have a strong constitution.
...*sigh*.
Fine. Lady Delphine has explained what happened.
It was a battle worthy of study in the war colleges, given the difference in strength between the two sides. Most of the people on your side weren't even regulars...
Oh? Should I be sad that no one will remember what we did?
You're wrong on that, Siege.
I'll remember. The soldiers outside will remember. The survivors of Chetleigh will remember.
Most legends are not born from official records.
I didn't know you were the type to say things like that.
Ahem.
Well, I've wrapped your wounds again. Don't try to blow me off with excuses like 'strong constitution', because I'm going to be keeping a close eye on your condition. I'm the doctor in this room.
I'll be checking on your friends too, and the injured soldiers and civilians.
Thanks, Lieutenant.
Also...
I suggest you check on that guest. I'm not sure if Lady Delphine is ready to handle her on her own.
I sincerely apologize for my behavior before, and my misplaced assessment about you.
There is one thing on which I won't budge, however. You're too impulsive and emotional.
You shouldn't be trying to solve every problem on your own. Turn around once in a while, and you'll find plenty of people ready to help.
*Glug* *glug*— whew!
Take it easy, Aunt Manning.
The Sarkaz were everywhere on my way here! I thought I was going to die.
Thank goodness that Lieutenant Shearer was tracking Irnclad Galavae, which was how he got there in time to save my life.
He's a decent man, but lacks flexibility. Those men would never allow him to board the ship.
You said you escaped Irnclad Galavae? What's happening there?
It makes me angry just thinking about it! Those damned officers charged into the central hub of the fortress and pointed their swords at me! Marquess Vontaire must be behind this!
I found an opportunity to change into inconspicuous clothes and escape. The fortress elevator almost ripped my dress!
Sounds like you really were lucky.

I thought I was just coming along with your mother to see how things were going on the front lines, and to take you home. What's the worst that could happen, safe inside a nomadic fortress? Who would have thought...?
The old Dukes of Windermere would be proud of Mother.
You're right.
But you're the Duke of Windermere now, Delphine.
Auntie, you're hurting me... what's that look on your face?
The general staff were eager to go back because... they believed the Duke's death was caused by a Leithanian plot.
You want to laugh, don't you? But they have evidence. Leithanien may not be in league with the Sarkaz, but they undoubtedly caused this situation.
What? Leithanien?
The staff are cautious about accusing Leithanian nobles directly, but they have set their sights on the rebellions going on the provinces.
No one wants to come all this way to fight the Sarkaz, only to return and find the demesne they have defended for centuries has fallen under Leithanian control.
You're her daughter, Delphine. Your duty will come soon.
Now, let me rest... then we'll leave this place.
Leave? To where?
You should not be with these peasants.
Watch your words! Don't call them peasants!
Alright, Delphine, listen.
If you really care about them, really think that they're heroes, a part of Victoria...
...Think of how many people like them are there in your mother's demesne.
If you really want to protect these people, take my advice and seek aid from the Duke of Caster to return to your demesne and take charge.
Perhaps you will have to become a Caster vassal... but you'll be able to fulfill your duty.
I—
What are you balking at? Did you fall in love with them just because they fought alongside you? You've stopped loving your own subjects?
Accept it, Delphine... you and your mother have suffered enough.
Accept Duke Caster's proposal, and at least Windermere will be spared from devastation.
Why do you hesitate? Do you really want to be a vagabond forever, leaving your family, your bloodline and your duty behind?
...I understand.
I'm Mother's daughter. You're right, I have a responsibility, not just to my friends, but to the subjects of Windermere as well.
But...
Why did the Swordguards leave me behind, when I still had such a duty?
If this was a duty glorious beyond all doubt, why do you come to me in such a condition?
You're right. If Windermere has fallen under the cloud of conspiracy, then I feel both the obligation and desire to do something about it.
But I...
We're with you, Delphine. Say what you want to say.
Sorry. I couldn't hold back anymore.
Do you know what we just went through, madam?
And you are...?
I'm not going with you.
You have a duty!
I'm my mother's daughter, comrade to these warriors, and nothing else.
Even if I once was... I don't believe it anymore.
I stood here for them, so that their lives would no longer be the playthings of people like you, and I'm not going anywhere until I've bled my last.
Leave.
I hope the Sarkaz don't take you.

lines.st: The Viscount's face is a spectacle of color, but Delphine gives her no further chance to speak.
She leaves with her head bowed in the direction of Lieutenant Shearer.
Delphine's expression is calmer than I thought it would be. What she said... what she went through...
It made me think of an old friend.
Delphine did not make the same mistake. Tragedy will not strike again.
But what was the price?
The death of a loved one. A swift betrayal.
Apparently, I was rather concerned how Delphine would react to these things.
Delphine smiles at me. It seems she has settled down.

lines.x: I'm fine. Let's go.

lines.st: The same thing cannot happen again.

lines.x: Head west once you leave the camp. If you're lucky enough, you should be able to reach the kind Duke of Caster.
Do you think you can survive without the Duke's protection?
We can, and we will.
Alright... I'm going to Duke Caster. I'll take a leisurely pace. This is your last chance.
Come with me, and we'll all live.
I won't stop you.
But the work of Delphine and her mother...
No, I'm not the one who's wrong... you're naive, stupid, foolishly believing that you're right.
So be it.

lines.st: I see her off on a vehicle not meant for rough terrain, leaving under the cover of darkness with a small escort.
I turn around to look at our group.
Hundreds, thousands of eyes converge upon a single spot. Some are soldiers laughing with each other, others are civilians, celebrating.
The camp falls silent.
They are waiting for an answer.
Mine.

lines.x: You heard her. It was undoubtedly an attractive proposal for many people.
We survived the worst of times together, Vina.
But what now? What happens when the morale and spirit that burns so brightly now is worn away by hardship and suffering?
Who will tell them where to go?

lines.st: I do not answer, not yet.
...He's here.
A creature with golden mane walks amongst the crowd. He looks at me.
I wanted to see the answer in his eyes, hear a word of advice in these fleeting moments.
But he gives no answers. All he does is look at me, waiting for me to find the answer.
Yes. Perhaps he is not even there.

lines.x: Lend me your ears.
If any among you still have faith in the proud dukes that are the faces of our Victoria, you should go with that woman now.
No one would fault your decision.

lines.st: The crowd rustles, but no one leaves.

lines.x: None among you?
Is it not our instinct to seek protection from the powerful? It's nothing to be ashamed of.
I would very much like to. Oh how I wish that this sorry sword I carry had the power to protect my friends, such that none among them would die.
She would very much like to. How many times has she cried when no one was watching? Wishing that someone could have saved her mother's life.

You would very much like to. Don't look away, I know you would. The Sarkaz wouldn't have butchered your brother so badly you couldn't even identify his corpse, if the troops that were supposed to escort you didn't flee.
But none among you have left.
You know that those nobles don't care about you in the slightest.
But do you really need to seek the strength and protection of another?
It's always been like that for us. We keep worshipping people and things that never really gave us anything, become their willing slaves.
Massacre, infection, besiegement. We survived all that, on our own strength!
We all want to go home. We all want this rotten war to end. But we can't win by hiding.
The fastest way is to give those warmongering bastards hiding in Londinium the old one-two in the face!
If the dukes won't do it, we will!
If no one will take us home, we'll walk home ourselves!
A gift fell into our laps, a unit ID that the dukes left behind in Gaul.
It was called the Exemplars. They were elites, the will of the nation, with a long list of accolades.
But now, they are nothing more than this little box in my hands.
It has no special powers. But we can take it back to Londinium—
Punch those warmongering bastards in the face, throw this in the faces of the dukes, and tell them—
This bloody land is OUR land!

lines.st: I want to go home, to the gym where my friends were.
They want to go home, to the lives they once had.
We cannot rely on others anymore. We cannot expect justice to be served on its own.
I smash my hammer into the earth with all the strength I can muster.
I do not know why I did it. All I know is that I can no longer hold back my anger.
Bam!
The hammer silences all other sounds.
'The Exemplars!'
It's the Glasgow Gang that first breaks the silence.
'The Exemplars!'
Then, they are joined by Delphine.
'The Exemplars!'
Until everyone joins in.
'The Exemplars!' 'The Exemplars!' 'The Exemplars!'

lines.x: Say, I had a strange dream—
I dreamed that a golden beast was taking a walk through our camp.
Good omen, perhaps?
Let's hope it all goes smoothly.
Wonder if the Self-Salvation Corps arrived at Brentwood before us...
We'll have even more hands once we join up with them.
Who would have thought, a few days ago, that we would have our own army?
I kind of like the name the Exemplars.
Are you sure about this though, Vina? Are we bringing all of them back to Londinium?
Very few of them are ready to fight the Royal Court head-on.
Perhaps...
There will be more sacrifices along the way.
I'll protect them.
Or maybe there will come a day when they don't need anyone's protection anymore.

lines.st: How absurd it is that we're chasing illusions on this battlefield full of bones, blood, smoke and mud.
The illusions are our only clue. Like the Lich said, the drifting fragments of history are the tracks it left behind.
We passed through many ruins, some long-forgotten, some never remembered to begin with.
Soon, these sights will be gone too. Many times we barely arrived in time to grasp the last traces.
We found seventeen Vampire War Arts Circles.
There are maybe dozens more... maybe even hundreds more of them on this battlefield.
They all appeared within the past week, a scary level of efficiency.
We found troop tracks on two of them, but the marks indicated that the troops were only on maintenance or patrol missions.
I doubt there's much point to destroying the Arts Circles. They could easily replace them with new ones.
It's only that much more important to take control of that thing, whatever it is.

lines.x: This place is completely ruined.
Just like the Rhodes Islanders said, it's another secret network of routes that lead into the city.
Through these hubs, Theresis has shipped huge amounts of steel, crystal units and witchcraft devices into Londinium, enough to repair The Shard and complete that airship.
But how did these materials reach Londinium? Even the dumbest dukes wouldn't let large shipments of materiel outside their control to travel freely around Victoria.
It's probably that they, like the Nachzehrer King's legion, appeared out of thin air around the hubs.
The so-called Sarkaz supply line we've been looking for may be the last and least important step of the transport process.
How did they do it?
Not only that, if Theresis had access methods like this, why isn't he using them directly in battle?
Teleporting armies behind enemy lines, or plopping Originium bombs right on top of ducal flagships.
Either there are limitations to the method... or it's actually really fragile.
The Lich called it an Art beyond the realm of Originium Arts, but I just couldn't picture it...
W, over there—
Huh?
Uh, no sign of battle. It's completely collapsed.

lines.st: W has looked detached ever since that encounter a few days ago.
I saw her gaze absent-mindedly into the fire while on night watch.
I can't help her. She'll get through this on her own.

lines.x: Let's go. There's nothing to see here.

lines.st: I look at the map in my hand. Scattered dots mark the locations of the Vampire Arts Circles.
I added a few, including the logistics hub just now, and the Nachzehrer King's legion camp.
Something had been placed there using the same methods.
There must be something linking them.
I scan the names on the map. Hills, valleys, small patches of forests, and a few small ponds.
Some sort of commonality? Shared geographical traits?
My eyes fall upon the name of a town. Brentwood.

lines.x: Ines, you said that Rhodes Island analyzed the intel from the Defense Forces command tower, where they had set their sights near a town by the name of Brentwood.

lines.st: I draw a circle around Brentwood's location.

lines.x: Closure came up with the analysis. She stole the records of all cargo movement since Theresis entered the city in 1094, and created an overlapping route map.
All the towns around Londinium were covered in these cargo movements on the map.
It's Victoria's capital, after all. All the steak and wine the citizens enjoyed before the war didn't grow on the shelves.
But the area around Brentwood is strangely empty, with only a few scattered entries around 1094 and 1095.
It's pretty weird. Like there's a forgotten town right here in the middle of Victoria.
The Rhodes Islanders think Brentwood might be the starting point of the Sarkaz underground railroad, or the hub of a hidden network, implementing some sort of secret embargo.
We just proved the existence of these logistics hubs.
But, we also learned that Theresis has a pair of, well, invisible hands, which lets him puts things anywhere he wants.
We may need to reassess the significance of Brentwood.
The closer we get, the more Vampire Arts Circles we see.
Meanwhile, if we draw a line from the furthest points to Brentwood, we see that they're about equidistant. That might be the logistical limit.
Stop guessing. I know Brentwood. You couldn't hide anything in that poor-ass town. There were barely any Sarkaz vagrants when I visited a few months ago.
Maybe the caravans just don't pass through 'cause there's nothing interesting there.

lines.st: W disinterestedly throws a rock over.
She's in a bad mood alright. She would normally have thrown a grenade pin.
The rock rolls on the map and falls right where I'm looking. I'm about to move it out away—
There's a patch of hills not far behind Brentwood. The rock fell right in the middle of the icons representing hills.

lines.x: ...*sigh*.
Let's go. It's not like we have any better leads. Maybe coincidence has something in mind.
Let's start there.

lines.st: The hills are dark and eerie.
A Victorian history book wrote that the study of history is like walking into a cave, feeling the grains on the wall.
Working blindly in the dark, all you can do is try to piece together the puzzle of where we came from by feeling around in the darkness.
This land is not friendly to history.
Originium veins assimilate any organic tissue around them, even bones.
All that archaeologists had to work with are Originium-infested ruins. All they could do is mull over metal shards.
But we are different. We are Sarkaz.
We have many ways to look back. Legends say that the revenants in the furnace of Kazdel whisper the achievements of a certain King of Sarkaz, or the sad ending of another.
When I was little, I used to wait by the furnace that never went out, hoping to capture a single echo from the past.
But all I ever heard was silence.

lines.x: You might actually be right. There's something unusual about the shadows here.
There seems to be a pattern in the chaos.
It's like the ripples created by a sailing ship, overlapping and interfering with one another, until nothing is left but entropy.
Not so much sailing as... drifting.
Drifting.
I once heard a story from an old Diαblo, this word came up in his broken whispers.
What story?
A story of disaster.
Let's hope we don't run into the stuff of those stories.
Let's hope we still have some good luck left in the bank.
A merc who thinks he's lucky is a dead merc.
Which is why we need you to stay behind, W.
We're not here to fight. We need to figure out what we're facing.
If things go south and we need to pull out...
Don't worry, I won't leave the way open for you.
I'll set up the explosives and wait until you're almost at the exit, Vampires and Nachzehrer hot on your tail, maybe Theresis himself, and just as you're about to see the light...
Boom!
You'll become the tragic hero right out of your favorite stories, Hoederer!
You can't handle Theresa on your own, W.
Ines.
I'll actually bury you, you know.
Hmph. You do you.
She really will blow the hills apart though.
We both know she's crazy. No need to provoke her with Theresa's name.
It's just pathetic, the act the she's putting on. I'd rather she knew what she wanted.
You've been in the city for so long. If she wanted to know the truth, she could've just asked you.
Did she ever ask if that Theresa was real?
She never asked me, either. She's never grown up.
She could do one of two things: Grovel at that Theresa's feet and beg for forgiveness, or plan to kill her.
But what is this that she's doing? Trying to prove that she's all great and powerful?
Or is she trying to convince herself that nothing ever happened, that all she needs to do is kill people and plant mines like she always has, and it'll all pass?
It would've been fine if she could actually stop thinking about it. But right now, she's like a little girl standing on the ice.
She takes a few steps forward, then a few steps back, looking around the same place, sinking into pointless sentimentality.
I have no idea.
Give me a break. You just don't want the extra trouble.
I didn't know you cared so much about her.
I care about me. I don't want to die because of her.
In all seriousness, Ines, have you ever thought about going back to Kazdel?
What? Am I in the way of your great plan?
No, it's just occurred to me that W and I have a reason to stay in this war.
But what about you?
...I've got my reason too.
Here we are.
We're... inside the hill.
They hollowed out the whole thing?
Well, that's going to make things easier for W's demolition plan.
Look at the size of these tracks, and the loading mechanism.
That's a big guy.
Shh, get into the shadows.
When's the next shift?
Close.
It's always on time.
I should've made a report to the boss. I would rather be outside, killing Victorians.
You're not a warrior.
I don't need you to tell me! It's just that this place is so... oppressive.
I see those illusions every day, the things you don't know whether they're real or not.
It's like a maelstrom that keeps spitting out trash from another dimension.
Good thing Ulšulah is a decent woman who doesn't pick on us for no reason or abuse her authority.
Is she the youngest major ever, according to Military Commission ranks?
She's not a member of the Royal Court. She won't go far.
Lady Theresa and the Regent aren't blue-blood devils either.
Screw the Royal Court. Kazdel wasn't won by those old fossils huddling around in a circle.
But they're the strongest.
That's because...
It's almost time.
Go to the machine and prepare to load.
Did you hear the name they said?
Ulšulah.
Could it be...?
It's not necessarily the one we're thinking of. Sarkaz names are often codenames or taken from legends.
Ulšulah the Time-Stealer. Ulšulah of the Lock and Key. It's a Sarkaz legend about the Feranmut. Anyone could've drawn from it.
Wait... Ulšulah the Time-Stealer?
What's the origin of your name, 'Hoederer'?
Don't have one.
Hah.
Bells.
Wait, over there...
What's that?

lines.st: What was that?
The hills are trembling...
No, space itself is trembling.
For an instant, I see visions beyond reckoning.
Bloated emperors crumbling in halls of gold, ancient bloodborn cutting open their wrists to feed the newborn.
Diαbolic flames burning amber. Demons with deer horns prowl beneath great bronze walls, offering battered willow wood.
The one knot falls from nothingness, splitting into countless threads; a red-skinned monster cuts off its own horn, impaling its own chest with it.
Broken shards become whole again. I see a far-away city.
Calm, peaceful, not yet wheeled, not yet drowned in hatred. Its beauty is absurd and out of place, even in a dream.
For no reason at all, I am convinced that it was Kazdel. A home that has long ceased to exist, from somewhere in history.
I want to go there, go into there. I want to see what kind of life we once led.
I lift my foot, but put it down in the same place again. I watch it quietly.
Until all is silent.
Until—
A pathetic creature that is naught but bleached bones moves slowly.
Nerve strands reach out and grab the massive containers.
It still roams this place. It is not dead yet.

lines.x: This... this is the 'cargo ship'?
So big... how did Theresis...?
It... it's really...
Ulšulah the Time-Stealer...
What a coincidence, right?
The legend of Ulšulah tells of Kazdel being attacked by a Feranmut that could slow the time of all things and peer into the past through the fog.
Through so many pasts the hero Ulšulah journeyed, rescuing their kin lost in the mirage of time.
Who knew that I, who took the name by happenstance, would one day witness the legend with my own eyes?
You've really ground those horns into shape, Ines. You could almost pass for a true blue Sarkaz.
Ulšulah.
Are you really going to betray General Manfred, Hoederer?
I knew you wouldn't kill Ines when I first heard the rumors, but the confidential nature of my work precluded a personal visit to Londinium to see you.
Aren't you supposed to be the handpicked successor to the Scar Market? Sounds like you're already the bone driver here.
Is playing a hero of legend worth such loyalty and commitment?
Ancient things are not so appealing anymore.
Only one stuck in the past would continue to use the name.
You could give yourself a new name.
How would old friends who came a long way to visit be able to find me then?
Care for a drink?
You didn't used to be a drinker.
Used to, huh?
You didn't used to swing your sword at me either, Ines.

lines.st: Bumping into an old friend isn't necessarily a good thing for a Sarkaz.
I may not be a Sarkaz, but I know to come prepared.
When you're a kid, you're naive and stupid. A little camaraderie is enough to get you handing over your heartfelt trust.
Even if the place where you built that camaraderie stinks as bad as Kazdel.

Eventually, at some point, you grow up. Maybe it's the first time you kill someone, maybe it's the first time you almost bleed out, or maybe it's the first time you crawled out from under a pile of corpses.

You start learning how to live in the grown-up world. You can be ruthless, you use any means necessary, you can even throw your friends under the bus just to live on, and nobody will hold it against you.
And then, that contrived plot comes around, and you bump into an old friend.
Honestly, after all these years apart, even if you're old friends, do you really have that much to talk about?
It's just... when you were young and stupid, you handed over that trust, you built a 'friendship'.
But when you're at that age, you have the unique opportunity to talk about 'friendship' in Kazdel without being laughed at.

lines.x: Ulšulah, we're done unloading. They're counting everything in at the warehouse.
Same as before: steel, pure gold, Originium, stuff like that. There hasn't been a personnel transport in weeks. Sounds like most of the forces from the Royal Court in Kazdel are already here.
That makes things easier on us. Back when the Royal Court guys were floating around, they'd come down here and complain to us, made all sorts of trouble.
Stop.
That's enough for now.
Oh, sorry.
Hey, uh, Commander, I've been posted to the waystation for six months now. According to the rotation schedule, it's about time for me to—
I haven't received any new orders for you.
But... your performance speaks for itself. I could approve three days of leave for you.
Ohh♪ Our Ulšulah♪
But don't let it go to your head, make sure to stay grounded.
All your life in Kazdel, you were a cobbler. Your greatest accomplishment is nailing soles together. What are you in the running for, unless you're prepared to blow yourself up?
Ulšulah♪ You're no fun♪
Get outta here, kid.

lines.st: I watch Ulšulah's profile, the corners of her mouth curling up.
Thinking back... back to then.
She carries herself the same way, at least.
The young Sarkaz cobbler approaches Hoederer and slyly hands him a flask.

lines.x: (Whispering) Hey, brother, hold onto this for me. Ulšulah can't go a day without the stuff.
(Whispering) You're really something, man, walking around with this piece at your side, and still—

lines.st: *sigh*.

lines.x: Watch your mouth, cobbler.
What's sweeter than honey♪ What's toxic as nightshade♪

lines.st: Looks like he hasn't even had his coming-of-age yet. Those years with Her Highness Theresa did a lot to see these kids grow up nice and sheltered.
And now, he's in a war out here by Londinium. Hah, brother and sister joined hands to create the worst joke in history.
If only it wasn't a joke.

lines.x: If you open that notebook, I'm setting it on fire.

lines.st: Hoederer quietly tucks away his notebook.

lines.x: You two haven't changed. That's nice.
It's been years.
Since that operation.
Me, Hoederer and W... the old W, got ourselves into trouble, killed some people we shouldn't have killed.
It's not just the Scar Market, showing your faces in Kazdel would be trouble for you.
I see your names there a lot... on the bounty lists. You're famous.
I've never been a cheap date.
Sure enough. Ines's investment paid off well.
We were all so young.
Getting old now?
I'm a Sarkaz, you're a sheep. In theory, I'm gonna live longer than you, Ines.
We're all Infected, so this isn't about racial lifespans.
Good point. I'll drink to you and wish myself good health.
——ahhh.
I remember you used to say that if a Sarkaz wants to live long, she needs to keep a clear head, before somebody else trades it for coin.
Whoever wants it can take it. Victorian, Sarkaz, doesn't matter to me.

lines.st: She leans in close, sizing me up, then glances at Hoederer.

lines.x: You've got wrinkles, Ines.
I died three times and only got a few wrinkles to show for it. I should thank the myriad souls.
You don't thank them, you're a sheep.
As for Hoederer, you're missing an eye.
Wanna see the socket? It's all thanks to the fine Caprinae woman right here.
Lose an eye, or lose your head. You had a choice.
I guess Ines broke her heart when she made the move.
...Drop it.
We're old friends getting together again, can't we get back to a nice atmosphere of sentimental nostalgia?
Talk about days gone by, muse on the passage of time...
We could talk about how you became the youngest major in the Military Commission, the driver of this skeletal Feranmut, Commander Ulšulah.

Yes... we should talk about that. Otherwise, we being who we are, we shouldn't be so warm and friendly.
And we could also talk about you, these two Sarkaz...
One Sarkaz traitor, and one sheep. Why are you popping up at a secret Military Commission site?
I know you were detained by General Manfred, Hoederer.
And you and Ines, who disrupted our operations, together with an obnoxious little girl planting bombs all around, stole into my territory, in broad daylight.
As an officer of the Military Commission, I can't very well let that go.
It's two against one here, Ulšulah.
Is it? Remind me, where are we again?

lines.st: Huh, since when?
The girl wasn't like this before.

lines.x: You're welcome to try, Ines.

lines.st: She's wearing a smile, but she's dead serious.

lines.x: The most important thing I've learned over the years is to be true to my decisions.
And Theresis is your decision?
No, the Military Commission is.
You decided to go with Babel—Or what is it called now? Rhodes Island? And I decided to stand with the Commission.
We decided our paths a long time ago.
Ulšulah, we can still—
No.
I know what you want, Hoederer, you haven't changed a bit.
But we're not those kids who strutted around cheery for a whole day on the back of a single bounty anymore.
We don't get to relax after our missions these days, no idle afternoons on the streets of Kazdel.
We are Sarkaz... or at least we're citizens of Kazdel. It's our responsibility to make those streets and alleys a better place.
Kazdel is the main issue here, Ulšulah!
Then you've gotta admit, the Military Commission is the best choice we've got! Even Her Highness Theresa wouldn't disagree!
It was the two royals who made Kazdel into a nomadic city, the both of them who made it possible for us to even talk about the future of the Sarkaz here!
It was the same two royals who promoted me, giving a poor girl from the slums a chance to stand in the sight of the Royal Court, among those pure and ancient bloodlines.
Once trapped by naivety and compromise, the Sarkaz lose our chance at a true homeland.
There's no room for me to hesitate here.
The Military Commission, it's... it's the foundation for Kazdel to become a nation.
Will there even be a Kazdel once this war is over?
All I know is I'm no traitor.

lines.st: Sure, we were prepared to bump into this old friend from the beginning.
Hoederer tacitly crosses from my side to confront this stupid girl, the Major of the Military Commission.

lines.x: Ten minutes, that's my limit.
Get as much intel on that thing as you can.

lines.st: I bypass several Sarkaz blocking my way, shadows entwining their ankles.
I leap up, climbing the nerve clusters of this terrifying creature, the slippery feeling making me slightly sick.
Is this a Feranmut? I've never seen one before.
Show me the secrets hiding in your mind.
This isn't a good section to walk on.
My luck has been pretty bad lately, always ending up in the body of some kind of monster. Last time it was the revenant, this time it's whatever this is.
Ugh, I hope I don't have to jump off it this time.

lines.x: These 'nails' piercing its spine must be some kind of witchcraft device.
Are those nerve clusters trembling? Does this thing still have a will of its own?
She's here!

lines.st: Ugh, how are they so quick? They climb up the skeleton in hot pursuit.
The sinews between the bones become something like a ladder for them, with the splinters of fallen bone serving as their weapons.

lines.x: Hm, they know war. Ulšulah's trained a real elite unit.
But they could stand to work in some variety.
All they've got on me is numbers—

lines.st: A foreboding instinct pops into my head and I instinctively dodge to the side.
A huge cluster of nerves comes crashing down, and the bone fragments I have been standing on shatter and crumble.

lines.x: It's so hard on itself.

lines.st: Hmph, looks like every bone fragment, every bundle of sinew here could be a deadly weapon. That's a pain.
The nerve cluster wraps around again. I sink my dagger into it so hard that it recoils violently, carrying me high into the air.
Alright, so it has the most basic of reflexes.
Good enough. There's a cabin up ahead.

lines.x: Right there!

lines.st: With all my might, I grab the dangling bundle of nerves and leap away.

lines.x: *cough*, *cough* *cough*!
Dust everywhere... from wind-weathered bone.
This should be the center.
Hm, no clear interface. Is it driven purely by witchcraft?
A willing slave? Or a marionette controlled by strings?
Hmmm...
You dodged my Arts, Ines. That should've gone right through your heart.
You still haven't fixed that old habit of yours, your casting always deviates a little to the left.
Lucky I've been on the bad end of that enough times.
Hmph...
You in a hurry? Did Hoederer make a fool out of you?
It's fragile, isn't it? Not as honest as it seems.
You're welcome to guess.
If we get the chance, I'd really like to hear about those three deaths you mentioned. Must be a thrilling story.
You wanna try once?
No, I'd rather us live.
If you can make it through the chaos and live on, that is.

lines.st: A tremendous force flips me over, the nerve bundle sweeps away, trying to tug me with it. The Feranmut is gradually swinging its body.
They've built a lot of cabins here.
And the thing tends to drift in the space.
What if the Feranmut wanders when I'm not in one of the cabins?
I really don't want to find out.
I'm not Hoederer, I'm not into becoming history.
Shadows, like blades, sweep through the hall of bone.

lines.x: Save what's left of your dignity, Ines, there's no point.
Your shadows can't protect you from the might of a Feranmut.

lines.st: Weathered bone dust takes flight again, filling my vision.

lines.x: Thanks for the reminder.
Hoederer—!

lines.st: Oftentimes, even I have to admit that Hoederer makes a pretty good partner.
I'm actually glad we didn't betray each other. It's hard to find a Sarkaz like him.
The past was tough, and the future's gonna be tough too.
The dim firelight rises with the dust, twisting.
His figure emerges from the embers, sparks in the wind singe his cloak and my hair.
And sometimes... only sometimes... I'm willing to tolerate his obnoxious Arts.

lines.x: We're well past the age when we can talk about friendship.
But I'd rather live in a future Kazdel that keeps the 'silly' naivety we always laughed at.

lines.st: The turbulence is intensifying.
I grab his hand.

lines.x: Sigh...
I forgot to tell you, Hoederer. General Manfred gave me your book.
It's pretty good, I have to admit.
You gathered up the bits and pieces, arranging them in chronological order, thinking you found the thread.
But you've been running all this time. Do you plan to do this forever?
Drop it, Ulšulah, time to say goodbye.
Hoederer, you're jumping with me this time.
Don't get swept away by the turbulence of history. Never forget the present... the life that you're living.
You're the boss.

lines.st: Boiling shadows point the way for us, scorched dust carrying us along.
The illusions shift before our eyes.
When I'm falling fast enough, I suddenly realize that history really does look pretty fragile and hypocritical.
I see a vast, and familiar battlefield. One of the war zones from Kazdel, where the flames are still burning.
I recall the past. Hoederer always wanted to steer clear of the war that tore apart his people. I force myself to turn my head, while he's single-mindedly focused on finding a suitable landing zone.
Run away from the war? Heh.
We weren't at Babel when Her Highness came under attack. We could have left Kazdel straight away. Why did we go back for W?
Why join Reunion? Why lay low in Londinium all this time?
What is Hoederer fighting for anyway?
What am I... fighting for?
We break through the earth, the illusions vanish, and we see what was always there.
He grabs my arm, telling me to be ready.
Arts envelop us and my vision clears. Shadows rise from the ground.
Maybe we stopped running a long time ago.
The myriad souls lie prostrate.
Howling knocks on the door from beneath, tangible usurps the intangible.
Qui'sartuštaj bears witness.
The Black Crown imprisoned, the offender perpetually innocent.
Because kings are born before immortality.

lines.x: Is W back yet?
No.
The time she's spent on this mission has already exceeded our worst-case calculations.
How's Nightingale doing?

lines.st: Shining looks past the window and at Nightingale, sleeping soundly under the trees outside.

lines.x: Not great.
Liz has never been more stable than she is right now, but the amount of Originium in her body is rising, and it's highly likely her nociceptors will be affected.
This can't be an ordinary Infection symptom.
Perhaps it's due to the Sarkaz witchcraft devices on the battlefield, or maybe the whispering of the revenant... I don't know.
There's no way I can tell Liz that 'I feel her pain'. For I have no idea what's going on.
But yesterday, she was happily telling me how she hasn't been feeling any pain in her dreams for a long time now...
You're hesitating.
You've been like this ever since you returned from the Confessarius's place alone.
Do you remember what you told me, when you applied to come with me to Londinium?
To 'heal Liz', Dr. Kal'tsit. This goal I've never wavered on, ever.
We may not know the exact status of her illness, but we all know what it means to 'heal' her.
The things we need to do will not change... on this I'm clear.
I have faith in you both.
The way you look at Nightingale isn't just with compassion. You're not just her doctor—she's healing your fears too.
Her body has its issues, but she's a member of the 'Followers', as well as your companion, and not a delicate flower that needs your protection.
The Confessarii secret is truly a sickening affair, but we might only be able to uncover the truth by facing the abomination lurking in the utmost depths head on.
I know you may be somewhat worried.
But as I said when we first agreed to work together, Rhodes Island will help you if you ever need a hand.
——Thank you, Dr. Kal'tsit.

lines.st: A blue fowlbeast chirps as it lands on Shining's shoulder.
This is Liz's usual way of greeting her.

lines.x: Liz's awake. I'll go fetch her.
I'm fine, Shining. You don't have to look after me so meticulously.
Liz, we were just discussing your condition, and there are some signs... that aren't too great.
The vicinity of Londinium has turned into a warzone, and there are some things that are affecting your health.
Dr. Kal'tsit and I are currently discussing our next course of action. This place is also just a temporary foothold.
Shining... come here. Closer.
Alright.
Look at you. Why wear that face when you've already made up your mind?
We can't run forever.
The way things are now, we might as well confront them with our dignity intact, right?
I know you'll stay with me, so I'd rather stay here, and be a little more proactive.
Liz...
Be strong, Shining. Your brows have been knitted ever since you met with them. Are you nervous?
Oh, right. While I was napping under the tree earlier... I had a dream.
I think we were children, walking in a stream...
The water didn't even reach our calves. It was cold, but very pleasant. We didn't speak, only walking along quietly.
And then I woke up. I saw a strange fowlbeast pecking at its own plumage.
It seemed to be pecking at its bruise-covered body until it was entirely covered in blood...
It gazed at me with a pleading look in its eyes, crying as it huddled in its own nest, but it was not liquid pouring down its face, it was white wax...
Even its mess of feathers were all frozen, and it was still chirping before it died—

lines.st: Qui'sartuštaj bears witness.

lines.x: It was a strange melody, that sounded like...

lines.st: Qui'sartuštaj bears witness.

lines.x: *Ancient Sarkaz Language*.
Liz...?

lines.st: Shining spots a black fowlbeast resting on the tree, weakly nesting on a crown made of dead branches.
It looks back at Shining, and a trembling cry resounds from its syrinx, a rhythm permeating—
*Ancient Sarkaz Language*.
A black feather floats from the crown of dead branches.
It covers everyone's vision despite its tiny size.

lines.x: You led me here.
And so I've come to take you home, Liz.
Mon3tr!
(High-pitched braying)
Return from whence you came.
The structure is so fragile. It seems Theresis's blade is sharper than I imagined.
Watch out, Shining!
It's fine, Liz. He can't harm me.
You've regressed.
It will be a challenge to return your body to its peak condition. That will eat into my experimentation time.
To think you'd willingly leave that sordid lair of yours...

lines.st: The black fowlbeast rests on the dead branches in the Confessarius's hand.
Its robust body is covered by an abundance of feathers, shining with luster.
It was reborn so easily and instantly from corruption.

lines.x: It seems like you're anxious about something as well.
We have already arrived at the gate.
We've made great strides in our experiments, even just knocking softly on the door.
You must attend the final ritual—
Mon3tr, meltdown!
(Angry howling)
Five minutes will be enough, Salus. Stop her with your life.
Do not let them hinder me.
Of course.

lines.st: A black curtain of satin drapes down.
It binds the howling monster in multiple layers, giving it less and less space to struggle.

lines.x: (Pained howling)
Her Arts are black...?
Is that... Amiya's? What have you done to her?!
They didn't defeat her.
I should have been able to present the King of Sarkaz to the leader unharmed. It's a pity.
But no matter. Even just getting a glimpse can let the fake take another step forward.
So, little creature. Don't disturb our leader at work—follow me elsewhere to wait till he's done!
(Angry wailing)
Mon3tr!
Dr. Kal'tsit, you're in no shape to exert yourself.
I said last time that our next meeting would be on the battlefield.
Oh? Then why are you concealing your sword even now?
You swore to sever your ties with our lineage when you left home, and this whole time I've been looking forward for you to return and fulfil your promise.
I gave you time, but you disappoint me again and again.
We were supposed to produce the next me. We would have created the most intangible bloodline in the last hundred years.
Ugh...!
Hm.
Sharp, but so impure. What's happened to your soul?
Did you think you still had hope to cling onto, after leaving us?
Liz!
Come back!
I won't...
Let you manipulate me!
Oh...? You're a creation that has surpassed my expectations.
You're supposed to be just a shell, and yet you can touch 'me'?
Now, Shining!
I... I can't hold on much longer!

lines.st: A strange feeling surges from within Shining.
Nightingale is resisting the Confessarius, and even suppressing him—the ancient devil's soul has never manifested in such a vulnerable state before.
Bloodline. The word itself sounds unreal, but at this moment there only seems to be an extensive crack of blood right in front of her eyes.
But Shining hesitates.
Why is this so simple?
How could it be this simple?

lines.x: Nightingale, stop!
It's a trap! He's waiting for your Arts to resonate!

lines.st: Time slows down.
Mon3tr fights with Salus as Kal'tsit yells out. Everything flows past like streams of light.

lines.x: Both you and I know where fate will lead us, but those without self-awareness will only trap themselves in their own pitiful cage.
It's futile to despise your bloodline and resist your fate. No one can deny the natural gifts bestowed by our bloodline.
I will do for you what you're hesitating to do, to crack an opening in the cage... Enter it with me, and I will bring you back onto the right track.
Think it through before you make your choice...
*Ancient Sarkaz name*.

lines.st: A white sword pierces though a heart atop a stone platform covered with dead branches.
Shining remembers the first time she cried, she remembers just how amazing it was, the birth of an unexpected emotion.
A brand new soul is carved anew under the blade.

lines.x: I've seen... pain... your... tears...
Accidents are bound to bring surprises. You could have stopped your explorations here and now, but you just can't bear to destroy the ignorant consciousness in that hollow shell.
And so you sew memories into the empty husk instead.
She's Liz. She shouldn't...
You did this of your own free will, filled up this consciousness with a name, with 'Liz'.

lines.st: 'Liz' is afraid of the completely silent person sitting across from her at the long table.
They agreed to sit here every night at the same hour for dinner together.
But the white-horned Sarkaz opposite her with a sword in her hands no longer speaks or moves outside of recording her every action she can remember today.
'Liz' always asks herself, have I done something to upset her?
'Liz' misses the beautiful memories, of how their lives were before...

lines.x: I keep having the same dream every day. I dream of us as children, walking in a stream hand in hand...
Then I flew upwards, and kept going higher and higher. You couldn't hold me back, and I saw you crying from the clouds.
Every time, when I wish to return to your side, I always wake up in pain.
I ache from your suffering, and I want to bear it for you.
I just need you to tell me why you feel like this...
Qui'saršinnag.
Qui'saršinnag. This name means 'inseparable hope'—our destiny.
Poor 'Liz'. She think it's all her fault, because of the momentary kindness you showed her.
I've already abandoned that sinful name. I'm called Shining now, and will always be.
Is that so? Are you sure you can resist your family's destiny that has carried on for a millennium?
I'm looking forward to it.

lines.st: 'Liz' holds the blue fowlbeast rescued from the river in her hands.
The indigo feathers are trembling, and the cold river water makes her shiver.
She thought Qui'saršinnag would get angry at her, but the sorrowful Sarkaz merely weaves a brand new birdcage for 'Liz'.
'Put it inside, and it'll stay safe always.'

lines.x: I'm sorry, Qui'saršinnag. I didn't mean to endanger myself by going into the river to save it...
But I just couldn't watch it die.
I became fond of it the moment your family gifted it to me.
It likes to fly freely, but once it gets too far away from me it seems to lose its life, and falls into the river...
You say I did something similar yesterday... but I have no recollection of that at all.
But I'll listen to you, and put it inside the cage. I'll keep it safe.
Please don't get angry. I promise you something so foolish as this won't ever happen again.
The cage has finally accepted their own duty, and you have also realized your own destiny.
The more fragments and lies you fill her up with, the more confusion and suffering she goes through.
I've lifted those dusty fragments for her. There's no undoing her awakening, and eventually, she will suffer as her rootless consciousness crumbles.
I won't let that happen, Father.
I'll end our sins with my own hands, and Liz will be 'cured'.
And what next? Isn't her regaining the truth the very thing you fear, my dear sister?
This is the source of your woeful hesitation.
I tolerate you wandering around with this receptacle, continuously strengthening this fragile cage...
Because I trust you to cherish it with your all, and you never disappoint me.
Eventually you'll come back to my side with the cage in tow.
That's what you've done. Look, She even tried to cage me in, to create an opportunity for you to defeat me.
Your rebellious love for her, and her fate resisting conscience have sadly only become another catalyst together.
You—
—Even want to use the hope she found herself?!
Shining? Are you here?
I can sense your breathing, but I can't see you...
I'm here, Liz!
She can't hear your voice. We're walking within her tattered consciousness—
—Inside the cage.

lines.st: Shining sees the blue fowlbeast, huge enough to fill her vision.
Giant gray eyes look down on her, she is unable to see herself reflected in those eyes.
The fowlbeast's sharp beak opens and closes, a mellow voice trails in from nowhere.

lines.x: Shining... there's a vague question echoing in my head together with a name that stings me, but I can't recall it at all...
I might be able to remember it after we leave this place.
I've found I can share the pains of others and slow down their lives withering away.
You said we'd go to faraway places to find a way to cure me, and maybe I can cure others in pain along the way too.
She's submerged in those disorderly and fragmented memories, and will eventually lose herself in them.
Or perhaps she'll go mad, or maybe she'll end her own life before that happens.
But you know how to solve this, Qui'saršinnag. I've taught you.
Draw your sword, and cut down your blood relatives here and now.
Our souls will reconnect with your sword as a bridge, and you—
Will give birth.
To ancient power.
This is your inescapable destiny.
Become one with me, and use the remains of your bloodkin to nourish her consciousness that will stay shattered otherwise.
Forgive me, Liz.

lines.st: Shining clutches the sword in her arms, and the Confessarius's hand is already pressing on the scabbard.

lines.x: At last, Qui'saršinnag...

lines.st: Day and night are about to pour out from the sheath.
But a single hand holds onto Shining's sleeve.

lines.x: Shining. I like your new name much better.
Liz...
I feel like I've slept for a long time, lost myself within an infinity of fragments.
But I heard your voice, and you guided me to a place buried underneath the countless fragments.
I dug up some of my splintered memories there... and I still haven't remembered them all.
But I've recalled the question that I should have given you my answer to long ago...
I forgave you long ago.
Even before you resolved to seal all the memories of that laboratory away...
So I'm angry with you for deciding to act on your own without asking me.
'Liz'. I've always loved the name you chose for me.
Don't run away and bear everything by yourself. Didn't we promise to face it all together—

lines.st: Shining sees Nightingale's eyes, and her smile.
When she comes back to her senses, the hand has already quietly slipped away from her sleeve.

lines.x: A tragedy we have here, but the ending has already been determined.
You killing me will turn you into the breeding ground for the next in our bloodline, or, you can look on helplessly as you watch me take her away and drown in your regrets after.
It's all the same, Sister.
Sir, we need to retreat!
Ugh!
How many bloodlines have you yet to touch, Chimera? Show me.
How many times can you put blood back into your body, and tell yourself your own fate?
(Confused growl)
(Angry howling)
(Excited cry)
Your little monster's copy is quite poor.
Is this because you're not as strong as you were before your rebirth, or is Dame Kal'tsit, beloved of all tribes, even more injured than I thought?
(Disdainful roar)
Qui'sartuštaj. The other part has chosen you.
So what will you bring him?
The name 'Qui'sartuštaj' itself is the answer.
(In ancient Sarkaz) 'Inextinguishable hope'.
Qui'saršinnag. The cage is already awake. If you continue to keep her by your side, she will only... like the dewdrops of early dawn, evaporate and fade away in the light.
I will take her away, and I will help you maintain her, I will help you make amends—
I will forge this cage into a crown.
And then, you will, we will be—
The King of Sarkaz for all eternity.
There's no point in chasing after him.
Take stock of our losses and prepare to move at once. We still have unstabilized wounded, but our cover here has already been blown.
Dr. Kal'tsit.
Operator Nightingale is an employee of the Rhodes Island Medical Department, and we will not allow anyone to take her away or to do her harm.
But we might place her in greater danger if we make any rash moves. After all, we're facing an immortal 'Lord of Fiends' that has been coveting the crown for thousands of years.
Qui'sartuštaj. The one who knocks on the door, the King of Usurping Kings.
I knew the threat of the Confessarii since the days of Babel, even though my numerous countermeasure attempts were all in vain.
Theresis concealed them so well that everyone merely thought of them as the regent's guards.
Please be at ease. I know what we have to do now.
I thought you'd be less calm than this.
What's your plan?
Liz... She has made her own choice.
A choice even I had never dared to think of, daring, aggressive, and also the most effective method. It seems like the one afraid of loss and death has always been me...
What I need to do. Heh.
Is to catch up to her.
...I believe in you both.
How's Amiya, Ascalon?
She and the Doctor were also beset by the Confessarii's surprise attack, and are now with Logos.

Amiya has been affected by some kind of witchcraft. The one who they call Salus tried to seize the King of Sarkaz's power from her directly and failed, but Amiya's current condition is... very strange.
She's fallen into a coma.
Amiya's connection with the 'Lord of Fiends' is only growing stronger, so we can only place our faith in her.
We also discovered several of the Vampires' Arts Circles, accompanied by some 'illusions' from history.
The Doctor mentioned that the Originium used to build these Arts Circles was very 'special'.
'Special'.
Well then, Lord Damazti, I thank you for your assistance. What do you seek from us?
We seek change. To hone ourselves. And meaning.
We seek the meaning of living a long and disordered life. You will show it to us, and we will verify it.
...That's all.
Tell Closure and Feist to sweep this place thoroughly. We'll leave in three hours.
Leave a message for W to head towards Brentwood as per our original plans.
We... really have to hurry.
Hey! Uncle Nowell! The lens in my glasses fell out again!
*Sigh*
I told you so many times your ears are too soft.
You grip on the frames so hard every time you feel your glasses sliding down that you break and change their shape. So of course the lens won't stay in place.
Let me have a look.
It's because you gave me such a heavy pair to wear!
But I found a better method, look!

lines.st: Little Susanna pulls out two leaves from behind her, and wedges the stems on her ears with the tips resting on her nose bridge.
Ah, glasses of green, fashioned with leaves.

lines.x: Ta-da! What'd you think?
Can you see with them?
I can! Really!
Just face the sun, like this—

lines.st: The child goes over to Nowell and puts two leaves on his eyes.
Nowell's vision is filled with a verdant green.

lines.x: Alright, kid, you—
Just look!

lines.st: Leaf veins.
The sunlight at noon in the forest is not very strong, but it is enough to shine through the thin leaves and reveal their interlaced veins, clear for all to see.

lines.x: How is it? You can see, right?
You won't see anything interesting with that frown always on your face, Uncle Nowell!

lines.st: He stares blankly at the sun with the leaves covering his eyes.

lines.x: ...I can't see anything at all.
But, I know of a story from Yan of the east, a tale of 'missing the forest for the leaves'—
Nuh-uh, not listening! You're as boring as ever, Uncle Nowell!

lines.st: The child with the glasses made of leaves comes and goes like a breeze, and disappears into the distant camp in the blink of an eye.

lines.x: It seems like you don't get along with the children, Mr. Nowell.
If it isn't Talulah. Done with work?
I told that story to my son once... however, he didn't like hearing my stories either. Maybe I'm just not suited for it.
You have an abundance of experience, even if we only know it from a smattering of phrases.
An average downtrodden Victorian optometrist who fled here after his shop was taken away from him amidst the war.
He sympathized with the Infected who were being discriminated against, and so chose to join their group and help out a little with his abilities.
You've always displayed a respectable character ever since I got to know you, Mr. Nowell.
But it's because of that... I...
Please speak your mind, Talulah.
I'm just curious why a long-lived like you would deign to conceal your identity and do such insignificant things.
I noticed. Of course, this is still much better than the worst-case scenario I thought of.
However... you can deny it, and we'll speak of it no more. I'll tell Nine though.
I won't.

lines.st: Nowell smiles, plucks a leaf and fiddles with it.

lines.x: There are actually many Victorians who I'm well-acquainted with, that know my identity... I only wish to avoid scaring my travel companions.
But I'd like to correct you on one point, Talulah. I'm not 'deigning' to act like this.
My longevity aside, I doubt I'm any more talented than ordinary people.
The long-lived gain their longevity through different means, as diverse as the Originium Arts they use.
But we aren't always influential figures, Talulah.
In fact... I'm much more interested in your story.
A descendant of the red dragon, founder of Reunion, and the leader of the Infected movement that took off in Ursus. To think you'd still act so shrewdly towards a so-called 'long-lived'?
...Who exactly are you, Mr. Nowell?
Just some guy.
A hundred and fifty years ago, I was telling my third son the fables I learned from my travels in Yan, and he left my side from impatience.
And as I stood before his granddaughter's granddaughter, I was once again a stranger in a large family.
So now I harbor anger and pain inside me, giving up entirely on both family and love as I try to locate the villain hiding in Victoria who manipulated my fate.
I spent the last century researching... researching the ''long-liveds', the 'gods', the 'masterminds', the 'immemorials', and myself.
I tried to benefit from the system of nobility, to hone my Originium Arts, to accumulate wealth and exchange it for secrets...
I tried every method you can fathom to get closer to the truth, but I haven't been successful at all.
I didn't manage to become nobility or wealthy. I only had a little shop... that's all I really had. Combat abilities wise, I might not even be able to defeat Mr. Guard.
Time has only taught me to recognize the reality that I hold no talents at all.
That's the story of an ordinary person, Talulah. This tale I can freely share with you.

lines.st: Nowell drops the leaves that were in his hand, and the breeze blows them far away.
He dusts himself off, and picks up his suitcase once more.

lines.x: Please, rest assured I hold no ill intent towards you all. I only wish to find... the 'hands of fate' that cursed me.
She hides behind the country, treating her monarch as her glove, and the city-state her chessboard.
She merely looks upon everything labeled 'Victoria' with a cold gaze. If needed, 'Victoria' can even be destroyed and rebuilt, and topped off with a new flag after.
How can I believe she isn't involved in our current turmoil of absurdity?
She's definitely hiding within the dukes. Watching the Sarkaz and Victorians engage in mutual slaughter. Bloodshed and sacrifice are merely heartless parameters and indicators to her.
Perhaps you might have been manipulated by a similar pair of 'hands' before—you have royal blood running through your veins after all. She has no reason to let you nor Alistair's daughter go.
Me? I do know another conspirator, but he doesn't deserve to be called a god, nor can he manipulate my fate.
But that's exactly how they live on, Talulah.
I want to know how to kill them.
Or perhaps, how to kill myself.
It's getting late, I need to fix little Susanna's glasses.
Mr. Nowell, you...
Talulah. The reason why I willingly tell you all of this, and why I joined Reunion—do forgive my selfishness—it is due to the intuition I've honed by the extra century I've lived.
You too, have resisted such a predetermined fate.
All of you here have.

lines.st: Where... am I...?
Pain, rip, shatter.
Every bit of suffering is piled upon my body, every person killed silently is myself.
No, I must...
But, why can I not summon the strength?
Someone seems to be urgently calling my name...
'Amiya!'
'You promised me—'
*Incoherent voices*
Is that the Doctor? I can't tell.
I want to reply. I try opening my mouth, just to tell the Doctor I'm fine.
The pain starts to fade away as the chaotic scene finally becomes clear.
I search for what strength remains in my body.
'I see cities, devastated.'
'I see Originium, blanketing the land.'
'I see you—'
The fuzzy echoes get further away as I open my eyes.

lines.x: This is... a battlefield.
My battlefield, the battlefield of the 'King of Sarkaz'.

lines.st: An army lies dormant behind me, silently raising their banners up high, soaked through with blood, pain and suffering...
...of ours... of our enemies.

lines.x: We will take this last city. Their resistance has reached an end.
You are... the king of the Nachzehrer Court.
What's your name?
I have no name. I am merely your loyal subject, 'King of Sarkaz'.
This is your fate. You will bring us salvation.
All that we fervently wish for, all that we have finally achieved... salvation.

lines.st: I nod slightly.
A droning cry fills the air, declaring a new chapter of destruction. The earth beneath my feet, it squirms, it curls, all because of me.
This is the grace the 'Lord of Fiends' has bequeathed the lands.
The formation begins advancing.

lines.x: Your will is our will.
You have answered the prophecy, and thus it will yield its rewards.
Please leave it to us. This battle will not drag much longer.

lines.st: Prophecy?
Prophecy.
I once heard that...

lines.x: You look exhausted these days.
Amiya, you can't go on like this. You need to rest.
...Theresa?

lines.st: Is that it? I'm... exhausted?
We fought a difficult battle at the Defense Forces' control tower, with Dr. Kal'tsit taking a serious injury.
Norport Borough has been locked down, all its residents left for dead.
We are at the tender mercies of Victoria's Catastrophes... But our military is yet to give up.
Within the Sargonian City of Gold, the Zhayedan millennium-old roars nailed shut in their coffins.
Zwillingstürme's spires rotted and crumbling, not a tune ever to ring out again.
The eternal holy city of Laterano, its pure white cathedral dyed in black, their ancient betrayal paid in full.
Even under the tides... The arrogant Ægir bow their heads to me. For those still try to uphold their dignity, I claim them one by one.
In all of these, one solitary figure remains constant.
Is all of this my doing?

lines.x: Is all of this... my doing?
These are all things you need to do, Amiya.
You have come to the origin of it all.
This is also our fated end.

lines.st: I feel something flowing out the edge of my eyes.
Theresa raises a gentle hand and wipes my tears away.

lines.x: Don't be like this, Amiya.
'King of Sarkaz'. I personally placed this crown upon your head, and brought to you this pain... and glory.
We will face it together.
This is... the will of the myriad souls. We cannot deny it.
The more you reject it, the more you suffer. The more you resist it, the weaker you are.

lines.st: Theresa's face begins to blur.
All of them are telling me this, all at once.
Across millennia, all the Sarkaz within the myriad souls, they are all telling me this.
There is no rebuke, no terror.
They just... truly wish for me to be at peace once more.

lines.x: We have seen so much defiance of fate. Prophecies manifest early before those who defy it, and it is clear as day where they will ultimately fall.
We are grateful for those pointless challenges. Through their struggles they weave an ode to heroes, yet the heroes themselves are destined to perish.
The more you reject it, the more you suffer.
Because you understand. All choices, all struggles, all fatigue, the sorrow you swallow, they bear no meaning. Rejecting it only embitters the gentle hand of fate.
The more you resist it, the weaker you are.
Because you realize. All fights, all resistance, the blood we spill, our haggard words, they have no destination. Resistance only fills your destined home with thorns.
Why bother... why bother.
We only wish for you to rest.
Close your eyes, Amiya.

lines.st: Close your eyes.
Millions of kin bow down before my rusted throne. They will bear Kazdel here.
The King spurs them, the King drives them, the King enslaves them.
This is your mission. This is my mission.
This is my... predestined fate.
'A good ending, is never earned.'
'This is fate. I tasted, too much.'
'I fought fate, so many years... in the end, I could not win.'
'But you... maybe you're...'
'Will you... admit it?'
'You're afraid, little King.'
'You know, my ending. You believe, all the prophecies, will be realized.'
'You will be, the origin of ruin. You will be, the harbinger of disaster.'
'You decide, you accept.'
'...Really?'
I—
Without exception, Amiya.
Even he, Buldrokkas'tee, succumbed to fate.
Are you stronger than him? Braver than him? Do you think those soft shoulders of yours can bear the weight of the frost and blades he's borne?
At the end of your journey, you will regret, you will suffer for your sin. Your sin of not being able to accept who you are!
Look at your hands, child. Look at your hands.
I glance down, feeling at my hands. I try curling my hands into fists.
Is this my will?
On the vast battlefield, I stand alone.
Innumerable corpses.
The Black Crown hovers above her head.

lines.x: 'I see you, black crown on your head, melting millions of lives, into nothing but memories.'
'I see the, King of Sarkaz, enslaving all peoples, everywhere.'
You will regret.
You will fall in regret, just like me.
The final destination... is still here.
When death approaches, you will realize, that all prophecies, are inevitable.
I... am the most horrific disaster to afflict our world.

lines.st: I am the most horrific disaster to afflict our world.
I step forward, towards Amiya, towards myself.
Towards fate itself.

lines.x: Then...

lines.st: Black Arts weave into a sword, stabbing the Amiya in front of me.
Stabbing myself through the chest.

lines.x: Then, I will kill you.
Even if, you are me, you are the 'Lord of Fiends'.
I once... made the same choice as you.
I thought that I was strong enough, powerful enough to unbind myself from fate's shackles.
You can't do it.
Amiya... you can't do it.
Really.
Maybe.
You may have tried once, you may have faced all sorts of bitterness and still fallen into the same abyss in the end.
But I haven't.
I have not walked on your so-called 'fated path'.
I will challenge you.
I will challenge you, in my, the King of Sarkaz's name, 'Amiya'.
If the end of it all was set in stone long ago by those prophecies you espouse, if the meaning of our lives are just to prove your inevitable conclusion...
Then why do we live?
Why do we exist?
The Patriot's lifelong struggle, Dr. Kal'tsit's assiduous choices, Dr. Doctor holding my hand and affirming our path—
All this just to become a footnote in fate's manuscript?
I refuse to accept it.
Amiya, this will only beget pain.
It will be, an endless torture.
Myriad souls of Kazdel, I ask you. Did Theresa break out of her own fate?
Even Theresis, did he face you head on?
Theresa... She handed over this crown to me, for this.
She didn't believe in the predestined tragic future that you paint.
So she is suffering now... Listening to our voices with nary a rest!
If only to relieve her burden, 'King of Sarkaz', you must—
No.
Theresa doesn't need any rescue.
The 'King of Sarkaz' will no longer be a symbol of conquest and revenge.
Maybe... that's why I must be the one.
How arrogant, how conceited—
No, it is only because I have worn this black crown, that I brandish my sword at you.
You cannot escape fate's choice. The further you run from it, the faster it will come—
I won't run.
I'll wait here for it. 'Fate'.
When your plans have dissipated, when your taunts fall short.
I'll show you all—
We... The peoples of this great land, can choose an ending for ourselves.

lines.st: I find the strength in my body.
They call this power the King of Sarkaz.
But I know, it was never about the King. This power was never meant for conquest, for subjugation.
Its true objective is—
'Survival'.
The battlefield abruptly shatters.
I hear a distant sigh.
It carries no anger, no fear, no sorrow.
Just a sigh.
Was this a reply from the myriad souls, or... just myself sleep-talking as I awake from a nightmare?

lines.x: Mm—
Amiya, you're awake!

lines.st: A familiar voice beckons. I feel those lingering emotions dissipate.

lines.x: Do you feel alright? Any pain?
You got hit by Confessarius witchcraft. Did you have a nightmare?

lines.st: The Doctor's voice always calms me down.

lines.x: I'm fine now.

lines.st: I lift my hand, the black Arts sparking out as usual.

lines.x: ...The 'King of Sarkaz'.
Doctor, this power is not a curse. It's not forbidden witchcraft that brings disaster.
Theresa passed it to me, so that... we can use it to find our own path.
I trust you.

lines.st: The Doctor holds my hands tightly.

lines.x: You're up, Amiya.
I'm sorry.
It's fine... Logos.
Whether or not I was caught by Confessarius witchcraft, I would have had to face this... self-reflection eventually.
In my dream, those voices proclaimed the prophecy again.
Prophecy?
Yes... The prophecy The Patriot spoke of before he died.
No matter what voices they were, it's all a trick of the Confessarii.
Don't worry about it.
No.
Was that a method of Confessarius torture, or are the myriad souls still deeply connected to my heart?
Or maybe I... still hold on to that scary prophecy deep down inside.
It's not important, though. I already rejected their offer.
Again.
...Good.
I still want you to get a full checkup from Dr. Kal'tsit, though. Your rings...
I do.
Um, Logos, where are we though...?

lines.st: The sound of footsteps approaches, it's... some Sarkaz mercenaries? They're moving a crystal shard the color of blood onto a truck not far away.
I recognize the girl leading them, Paprika. The Doctor said she freed the workers in Highbury.

lines.x: Logos requisitioned the use of this Sarkaz transport squad's vehicle. In the name of the Lord of the Banshees.
We need to escape the Confessarii pursuit.
Yesterday, Closure's drones found us. W's safehouse that they were staying at were also attacked by the Confessarii, and Nightingale was captured.
...The Confessarii.
